canalizing.def: 渠化、開運河、引導的人 (或物) (導)
canalizing.ex: Canalizing the water flow prevented further soil erosion.

canard.def: 謠傳、虛報、鴨子(俚)的人 (或物) (謠)
canard.ex: The story about his resignation turned out to be a total canard.

canary.def: 金絲雀、淡黃色、告密者、告密的人的人、淡黃地 (雀)
canary.col: sing like a canary (告密), canary yellow
canary.ex: The canary's cheerful song filled the quiet house.

cancellation.def: 取消、抹消、蓋銷(郵票)、作廢 (單數)
cancellation.ex: Due to the blizzard, there were several flight cancellations.

cancel.def: 取消、抹消、中和、抵銷、取消的人的人、取消地 (消)
cancel.ex: I'm sorry, I have to cancel our lunch date for tomorrow.

cancelled/canceled.def: 取消了的、作廢的人的人 (或物)
cancelled/canceled.ex: The game was cancelled because of the heavy rain.

cancer.def: 癌症、(社會)腫瘤、巨蟹座、毒瘤的人的人物、毒瘤地 (癌)
cancer.col: lung cancer, fight cancer, spread like cancer
cancer.ex: Early detection is key in the successful treatment of cancer.

cancerous.def: 癌症的、如癌症般擴散的
cancerous.ex: The doctor removed a small cancerous growth from his arm.

candelabrum.ex: A massive silver candelabrum stood in the center of the table.

candid.def: 坦白的、直率的、偷拍的、公正地 (坦白)
candid.ex: She gave me some very candid advice about my career.

candidacy.def: 候選人的資格、候選狀態 (單數)
candidacy.ex: He announced his candidacy for the local mayor's office.

candidate.def: 候選人、應試者、報名者的人物 (單數)
candidate.ex: There are three candidates running for the position.

candidly.ex: I'll speak candidly: your performance needs to improve.

candied.def: 蜜餞的、用糖煮過的的人 (或物) (甜)
candied.ex: She loves the taste of candied fruit in her fruitcake.

candle.def: 蠟燭、燭光、(解)光度、用蠟燭的人的人、蠟燭地 (燭)
candle.col: candle light, burn the candle at both ends (筋疲力竭), can't hold a candle to (比不上)
candle.ex: He lit a small candle to see his way in the dark.

candlelight.ex: They enjoyed a romantic candlelight dinner by the sea.

candlestick.ex: She placed the brass candlestick on the wooden mantelpiece.

candor/candour.ex: I admired the refreshing candor of her reply.

candy.def: 糖果、冰糖、蜜餞、砂糖、變成蜜餞、糖果的人的人、糖果地 (糖)
candy.col: candy bar, cotton candy, eye candy (養眼的人/物)
candy.ex: The children spent all their pocket money on candy.

cane.def: 莖、藤條、手杖、(英)體罰、用杖擊、手杖的人的人、手杖地 (杖)
cane.col: walking cane, sugar cane, cane chair
cane.ex: The elderly gentleman walked with the help of an ebony cane.

caning.def: 藤條處罰、被打的人 (或物) (罰)
caning.ex: A former era saw schoolboys being given a severe caning.

canine.def: 犬的、犬科的、犬齒、犬、犬科的人物、犬類地 (犬)
canine.ex: Wolves and foxes are members of the canine family.

canister.ex: The fresh tea was stored in an airtight kitchen canister.

canker.def: (醫、植)潰瘍、弊端、(口)腐蝕、潰瘍的人、腐蝕地 (弊)
canker.ex: Poverty is a canker that destroys the health of a society.

cankerous.def: 潰瘍的、腐蝕性的的人 (或物)
cankerous.ex: The deep, cankerous growth was difficult to treat properly.

cannabis.ex: The use of medical cannabis is a controversial topic tonight.

canned.def: 罐裝的、(俚)喝醉的、(口)預先錄製的人生的人 (或物) (罐)
canned.trans: can (名詞/動詞/助動詞/形容詞/副詞)
canned.ex: The sit-com uses canned laughter throughout the show.

cannery.ex: Many people in the seaside town work in the fish cannery.

cannibal.def: 食人者、食同類者、食人的、食同類的人的人物、食人地 (食人)
cannibal.ex: Historical accounts describe a savage tribe of cannibals.

cannibalism.def: 同類相食、食人行為 (單數)
cannibalism.ex: Some species of spiders practice a form of cannibalism.

cannibalize.def: 拆用零件、吃同類的人的人物、拆用地 (拆)
cannibalize.ex: The mechanic had to cannibalize old machines for parts.
